free_text: Survey of the (alpha,2He) reaction on 1p- and 2s 1d-
slits which were separated by a 10-mm-high central
post. At 11 cm from the target, this system subtended
a 15 degrees vertical and a 4 degrees horizontal
acceptance angle. For the gas target measurements, a
located at a distance of 4 cm from the center of the
gas cell. Each telescope consisted of a 380-mu-m
phosphorus diffused silicon deltaE counter, a 5-mm
Si(Li) E counter and a 5 mm Si(Li) veto counter.
From the authors. The excitation energy of 10.55 MeV
quoted in J,PRL,37,812,1976 is too low due to a
nonlinear behavior of the E detectors used in that
experiment. The excitation energy is equal 10.72 MeV.
From compiler. Cross section values for the 4+ state
for the excitation energy 10.72 MeV, presented in
J,PR/C,18,9,1978 is in disagreement with the previously
Fig.3 in J,PRL,37,812,1976 and Fig.5 in
